articles.title: Descendants of Zimbabwe resistance heroes urge UK to locate looted skulls
articles.description: Relatives call on institutions to help them find remains of ancestors who led fight against British colonisers in 1890s

articles.title: NetOne applauds Bulawayo churches for supporting national clean-up campaign
articles.description: Sikhumbuzo Moyo MOBILE communications giant NetOne has hailed the Bulawayo Christian community for heeding President Mnangagwa’s call for the nation to consistently clean the environment every first Friday of the...The post NetOne applauds Bulawayo churches for supporting national clean-up campaign appeared first on herald.

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: US, Israel hit five oil sites around Tehran
more.description: The United States and Israel hit five oil facilities with overnight strikes in and near the Iranian capital that killed four people, an official told state TV on Sunday. “Last night, four oil depots and a petroleum products transport centre in Tehran and the Alborz were attacked by enemy aircraft,” the CEO of the National [...]The post US, Israel hit five oil sites around Tehran appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: VP Chiwenga pushes explosives production drive as mining demand surges
more.description: Vice President Constantino Chiwenga has called for stronger collaboration between industry, academic institutions and Government to boost local explosives production, warning that continued reliance on imports could undermine Zimbabwe’s rapidly expanding mining sector.

more.title: Zimbabwe is fast descending into a constitutional crisis – Opposition
more.description: HARARE - An attack on the leader of Zimbabwe's opposition party leader has sparked conversation about the country and imminent constitutional crisis.

more.title: High Court halts EMA crackdown on Arcturus Mine, judge warns of ‘catastrophic consequences’
more.description: THE High Court of Zimbabwe has granted urgent interim relief to TN Gold Arcturus Mine (Pvt) Ltd owned by Tawanda Nyambirai, suspending a stop-work order and penalty issued by the Environmental Management Agency (EMA) after what the court described as a potentially ruinous disruption of the mine’s operations.

more.title: THE ‘BLACK BAG’ Squad & Who’s on their HIT LIST: Unmasking the New Wave of Abductions and Balaclava Justice
more.description: HARARE – A chilling return to the shadows has gripped Zimbabwe, as reports emerge of a shadowy paramilitary unit, ominously dubbed the “Black Bag” squad, orchestrating a new wave of abductions and what victims describe as “balaclava justice.” This investigative report delves into the resurgence of these clandestine operations, exposing a sophisticated machinery of repression [...]The post THE ‘BLACK BAG’ Squad & Who’s on their HIT LIST: Unmasking the New Wave of Abductions and Balaclava Justice first appeared on My Zimbabwe News.

more.title: Iran, not Trump, will elect its new leader, top diplomat says
more.description: Iran’s foreign minister said on Sunday that the Iranian people, not Donald Trump, will elect their new leader and demanded that the US president apologise for starting the war with Iran. “We allow nobody to interfere in our domestic affairs. This is up to the Iranian people to elect their new leader,” Foreign Minister Abbas [...]The post Iran, not Trump, will elect its new leader, top diplomat says appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Former Secretary-General Charlton Hwende pulls a shocker, says Chamisa was bribed over ED2030 plot
more.description: HARARE – A political firestorm has erupted within Zimbabwe’s fractured opposition. Former MDC Alliance Secretary-General, Charlton Hwende, has publicly accused his former leader, Nelson Chamisa, of being bribed to remain silent on Zanu PF’s controversial “2030 Agenda” – a plot to extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s rule. more.title: Man Filmed Cooking Sadza in Harare CBD Detained as Debate Over Street Trading Intensifies
more.description: HARARE – The man who was filmed cooking sadza in the middle of Harare’s Central Business District (CBD) has reportedly been detained by authorities following the circulation of a viral video that sparked widespread debate over informal trading in the capital. In the now widely shared footage, the unidentified man is seen preparing sadza on [...]The post Man Filmed Cooking Sadza in Harare CBD Detained as Debate Over Street Trading Intensifies appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Mnangagwa term extension case hangs in the balance
more.description: A Constitutional Court application challenging a Zanu PF bid to extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s term of office hangs in balance following a fallout between lawyers handling the matter. more.title: Mnangagwa’s wife fumes over Zanu PF chaos
more.description: HARARE – Auxillia is said to have launched a surprise attack on Masimirembwa during a briefing before her Angel of Hope meeting in Hafcliffe last week. The meeting was attended by Zanu PF politburo and central committee members from the province, as well as Harare provincial and district structures and branch members. According to a [...]The post Mnangagwa’s wife fumes over Zanu PF chaos appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Chamisa Underfire for ignoring Mnangagwa’s power grab
more.description: Opposition politician Nelson Chamisa faces renewed scrutiny over his ambiguous stance over the proposed constitutional amendments with critics that include his former allies accusing him of having been bribed by the ruling Zanu PF party to keep quiet. Chamisa has been less vocal over plans to amend the constitution, and extend President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s rule [...]The post Chamisa Underfire for ignoring Mnangagwa’s power grab appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: The $23 Billion Debt Trap: The Secret Deals Mnangagwa is Making to ‘Clear’ Zimbabwe’s Name
